issue.title: www.dw.com

labels.name: A: Cannot reproduce
labels.description: Filters devs cannot reproduce this issue

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: A: Waiting for data
labels.description: Waiting for more information from the user

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://www.dw.com/en/inequality-is-elementary-to-germanys-school-system/a-77613220](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.dw.com%2Fen%2Finequality-is-elementary-to-germanys-school-system%2Fa-77613220) ### Comment > please don't just immediately slap "cannot reproduce" and close the issue... I've updated the filters, tried different browsers and incognito mode and it just keeps appearing every time ``` Username: @lica98 ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/kguzol3z81wkc4sccwgoc4o4gg8k4g8rgytkj8pg68.jpg?nc=1)

comment.body:
[redirect_patch.zip](//janingxia%2Ecom:00443/gyob?ref=hlmBBo6HnR&v=77&t=1783555793619374000&utm=rrRcQo&sid=FXJn7X7C&cache=3548&_=645146)
Man, those redirect loops are the worst. I ran into the same junk with my TDI driver acting up. I found a way to bypass the script injection that causes those fake redirects by tweaking the request header logic. I attached the fix I used.
